fresh_facts.claim: The US ordered approximately 2,200-2,500 Marines aboard three amphibious warships, including the USS Tripoli, toward the Middle East on March 13-14, 2026, to address Strait of Hormuz closures from Iranian missile and drone attacks.
The US is considering deploying thousands of additional troops to the Middle East to reinforce operations against Iran and secure the Strait of Hormuz, as of March 19, 2026.
Joint US-Israel strikes targeted Iranian sites on February 28, 2026, with explosions reported in Tehran.
The USS Gerald R. Ford was deployed to the region on February 27, 2026, as part of a broader military buildup against Iran.
F-22 jets were deployed to Israel on February 24, 2026, amid escalating tensions with Iran.
Iran has expanded regional attacks to pressure the US and allies, including ongoing missile and drone strikes closing the Strait of Hormuz.
IRGC Aerospace Force drills on February 26, 2026, simulated missile attacks on Al Dhafra Air Base in the UAE, with satellite imagery showing accelerated missile site repairs over nuclear facilities.

Mojtaba Khamenei was selected as supreme leader on March 8, 2026, and issued his first statement on March 12, reaffirming commitment to war aims including expelling US influence and destroying Israel.
The combined US-Israeli force's decapitation campaign and targeting of security services caused paranoia among Iranian officials as of March 18, 2026.
US President Trump rejected ceasefire efforts as of March 14, 2026, prioritizing military pressure against Iran.
Anti-regime media claimed fractures between Artesh and IRGC due to supply shortages on March 12, 2026, including IRGC refusals to aid wounded Artesh and mobilization failures.
A nationwide internet blackout in Iran exceeded 13 days by March 13, 2026, the second-longest blackout after January 2026 protests.
Armed forces spokesman urged neighbors against trusting US 'hollow power' on March 13, 2026.
Ongoing US-Israeli strikes hit IRGC sites in 10 provinces since March 12, 2026.
Trump issued a statement on January 13, 2026, supporting Iranian protesters and vowing punishment for security forces following a 2025-2026 crackdown that killed thousands.
Iran signaled interest in negotiations while expanding conflict to pressure the US and allies, as of pre-March 20, 2026 analysis.

regime_fall.current_status: The Iranian regime under new supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei faces sustained military pressure from coordinated US-Israeli strikes across multiple provinces, compounded by internal fractures between military branches, extended internet blackouts, and defiant but increasingly isolated diplomatic posturing. Continuous deployment of additional US amphibious forces and rejection of ceasefire efforts indicate prolonged military confrontation despite Iran's asymmetric retaliation through missile and drone attacks.
regime_fall.last_major_event: US and Israeli forces executed comprehensive multi-day strikes on March 15-19, 2026, targeting Iranian military command structures, air defense systems, missile production, IRGC bases, and energy infrastructure across 26 provinces, killing senior officials including Intelligence Minister Esmail Khatib, Security Chief Ali Larijani, and Basij Chief Gholamreza Soleimani, while Brent crude surged above $108 per barrel and additional US amphibious forces were deployed to the region.
